headline: Aperture Raises $140M Series B as Co-Founders Scale Multimodal World Models for Enterprise Simulation
description: Co-founded platform building multimodal world models that simulate real environments so agents can rehearse logistics, robotics, and ops workflows before touching production systems.
founderQuote: Agents that only train on text hallucinate the physical world. We give them a camera-ready universe with physics and failure modes.
investorQuote: World models are the next infrastructure layer after foundation models—Aperture is shipping enterprise-grade simulation, not research demos.
xPost:
Aperture raises $140M Series B. Co-founded platform building multimodal world models that simulate real environments so agents can rehearse logistics, robotics, and ops workflows before touching production systems.

"Agents that only train on text hallucinate the physical world. We give them a camera-ready universe with physics and failure modes." — Lin Wei & Chris Novak

"World models are the next infrastructure layer after foundation models—Aperture is shipping enterprise-grade simulation, not research demos." — Sarah Tavel · Benchmark
